message.content:
## Task: Add regime operation, provenance, and hierarchy terms to AXLA-Lexicon.adoc

Edit the file `Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/AXLA-Lexicon.adoc`. This is the master lexicon for the concept model system. You must read the file before editing.

**IMPORTANT**: This file uses MCM normalization — linked terms like `{axrg_regime}` must be isolated on their own lines when they appear in prose. Follow the existing patterns exactly.

### Part 1: Mapping Section Additions

After the existing block (around line 106):
```
:axhrgv_variable:                     <<axhrgv_variable,Hierarchy Grouped Variable>>
```

Add these new mapping entries:

```
// Axial Regime Provenance Dimensions (assignment origin)
:axrd_file_sourced:                   <<axrd_file_sourced,File-Sourced>>
:axrd_env_inherited:                  <<axrd_env_inherited,Environment-Inherited>>
:axrd_constructed:                    <<axrd_constructed,Constructed>>

// Axial Voicing Regime Operation Terms (definition-site annotations)
:axvr_broach:                         <<axvr_broach,Broach Voicing>>
:axvr_validate:                       <<axvr_validate,Validate Voicing>>
:axvr_render:                         <<axvr_render,Render Voicing>>

// Axial Hierarchy Regime Operation Markers (subdocument)
:axhro_broach:                        <<axhro_broach,Hierarchy Broach>>
:axhro_validate:                      <<axhro_validate,Hierarchy Validate>>
:axhro_render:                        <<axhro_render,Hierarchy Render>>
```

### Part 2: Provenance Dimension Definitions

Add a NEW section after the existing "Axial Regime Terms" section (which ends around line 1877). Insert BEFORE "== Axial Regime Annotation Terms" (around line 1879). The new section:

```
== Axial Regime Provenance Dimensions

Provenance dimensions qualify
{axrg_assignment}
to declare how values arrive at
{axvr_broach}
time.
These are valid dimensions on
{axvr_regime}
declaring which provenance(s) a regime supports.

[[axrd_file_sourced]]
{axrd_file_sourced}::
Assignments loaded from a persistent file via source or equivalent mechanism.
The file path is known at load time and the regime module is responsible for locating and sourcing it.

[[axrd_env_inherited]]
{axrd_env_inherited}::
Assignments received as environment variables from a parent process.
The regime module finds variables already in scope; no file sourcing occurs.

[[axrd_constructed]]
{axrd_constructed}::
Assignments computed or set programmatically before broach.
Values are assembled by application logic rather than loaded from a static source.
```

### Part 3: Voicing Operation Definitions

Find the existing section "=== Definition-Site Voicing Annotations (axvr_*)" (around line 1884). After the last existing voicing definition (`axvr_group`, which ends around line 1947), add:

```
[[axvr_broach]]
{axvr_broach}::
Voices a regime broach operation definition.
The annotated term defines the silent initialization phase for a
{axrg_regime}:
setting defaults, detecting unexpected variables, and building derived structures.
+
The definition text must include, as its second
{mcm_attribute_reference},
the parent regime.

[[axvr_validate]]
{axvr_validate}::
Voices a regime validate operation definition.
The annotated term defines the strict verification phase for a
{axrg_regime}:
checking all type and constraint requirements, failing on first violation.
+
The definition text must include, as its second
{mcm_attribute_reference},
the parent regime.

[[axvr_render]]
{axvr_render}::
Voices a regime render operation definition.
The annotated term defines the diagnostic display phase for a
{axrg_regime}:
producing human-readable presentation of current state.
Rendering occurs before validation so all fields are visible on failure.
+
The definition text must include, as its second
{mcm_attribute_reference},
the parent regime.
```

### Part 4: Hierarchy Marker Additions

Find the marker table (around line 1973-1982). Add three new rows to the table AFTER the `axhrgv_variable` row but BEFORE `|===`:

```
|{axhro_broach}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level broach operation for this regime
|{axhro_validate}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level validate operation for this regime
|{axhro_render}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level render operation for this regime
```

Then find the section where individual markers are defined (after the table, starting with `[[axhrb_regime]]`). After the `[[axhrgv_variable]]` definition block (which ends around line 2028), add:

```
[[axhro_broach]]
{axhro_broach}::
Marks a regime broach operation description in a subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the kit-level broach operation term (which must be voiced
{axvr_broach}
in the parent document).

[[axhro_validate]]
{axhro_validate}::
Marks a regime validate operation description in a subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the kit-level validate operation term (which must be voiced
{axvr_validate}
in the parent document).

[[axhro_render]]
{axhro_render}::
Marks a regime render operation description in a subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the kit-level render operation term (which must be voiced
{axvr_render}
in the parent document).
```

### Part 5: Update Nesting Rules

Find the "==== Nesting Rules" section (around line 2030). Add after the existing rules:

```
* {axhro_broach},
{axhro_validate},
and
{axhro_render}
must appear within
{axhrb_regime}
scope but outside any
{axhrgb_group}
scope
```

### Part 6: Update Regime Specification Completeness

Find "=== Regime Specification Completeness" (around line 2557). After the existing rule about `axf_format`, add:

```
* Provenance dimension (
{axrd_file_sourced},
{axrd_env_inherited},
or
{axrd_constructed})
declaring how
{axrg_assignment_s}
arrive
```

## Wrap Discipline

DO NOT run jjx_close or mark the pace complete. When work is done:
1. Verify each edit by reading the modified sections back
2. Report what was added and where
3. STOP

   302→
   303→== Core Principles
   304→
   305→The Axial Lexicon provides reusable, atomic terminology for technical specifications.
   306→Each term maintains precise semantics while adapting to implementation context.
   307→Specifications selectively import only needed axial terms, avoiding vocabulary bloat.
   308→
   309→== Motif Vocabulary
   310→
   311→AXL terms are
   312→{axl_motif_s}
   313→- abstract patterns that concrete specifications voice in their local context.
   314→
   315→[[axl_motif]]
   316→{axl_motif}::
   317→An abstract pattern defined in the Axial Lexicon that can be voiced by concrete terms in specifications.
   318→A
   319→{axl_motif}
   320→captures the essential shape of a concept while remaining independent of implementation context.
   321→Specifications voice
   322→{axl_motif_s}
   323→through local terms that bind the abstract pattern to specific environments.
   324→
   325→[[axl_voicing]]
   326→{axl_voicing}::
   327→A concrete term in a specification that gives local expression to A
   328→{axl_motif}.
   329→Each
   330→{axl_voicing}
   331→adapts the abstract pattern to a specific context while preserving the
   332→{axl_motif_s}
   333→essential character.
   334→
   335→[[axl_voices]]
   336→{axl_voices}::
   337→The relationship where a concrete term expresses a
   338→{axl_motif}
   339→in a specific context.
   340→When a term
   341→{axl_voices}
   342→a
   343→{axl_motif},
   344→it gives the abstract pattern local form and implementation-specific behavior.
   345→
   346→[[axl_evokes]]
   347→{axl_evokes}::
   348→The relationship where A
   349→{axl_motif}
   350→calls forth its
   351→{axl_voicing_s}
   352→in the reader's understanding.
   353→When encountering A
   354→{axl_motif},
   355→it
   356→{axl_evokes}
   357→the concrete realizations known from various specifications.
   358→
   359→[[axl_annotation]]
   360→{axl_annotation}::
   361→A
   362→{xref_MCM}
   363→comment using Strachey brackets that declares the
   364→{axl_motif_s}
   365→a term
   366→{axl_voices}.
   367→The
   368→{axl_annotation}
   369→appears between anchor and definition as a space-separated list of AXL terms within Strachey brackets.
   370→+
   371→Format: `// ⟦relationship primary [dimension ...]⟧`
   372→+
   373→* First term: the relationship (typically `axl_voices`)
   374→* Second term: the primary
   375→{axl_motif}
   376→being voiced
   377→* Remaining terms: dimension terms (prefixed `axd_`) specifying characteristics
   378→+
   379→Examples:
   380→+
   381→* `// ⟦axl_voices axc_call⟧` - simple voicing
   382→* `// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_nullable⟧` - voicing with dimensions
   383→+
   384→Category prefixes carry semantic meaning, eliminating need for punctuation.
   385→Position determines role: relationship first, primary second, dimensions following.
   386→This design prioritizes LLM interpretability over human visual parsing.
   387→When ambiguity or friction arises during LLM processing, the format should evolve to resolve it.
   388→
   389→== Motif Implications
   390→
   391→{axl_motif_s} carry implied characteristics beyond their definitions.
   392→These implications guide how
   393→{axl_voicing_s}
   394→should behave:
   395→
   396→* Anchor placement expectations per
   397→{xref_MCM}
   398→form patterns
   399→* Type/instance level - where the
   400→{axl_motif}
   401→sits in abstraction hierarchy
   402→* Contextual requirements - parent structures or operations the
   403→{axl_motif}
   404→exists within
   405→* Voicing expectation - whether the
   406→{axl_motif}
   407→typically requires local voicing
   408→
   409→== Form Expectations
   410→
   411→Each AXL category implies an expected
   412→{xref_MCM}
   413→definition form pattern for documents using that category.
   414→During type-scheme linting, these expectations guide validation of definition structure.
   415→
   416→* {axc_*} control terms expect
   417→{xref_MCM}
   418→`mcm_form_deflist`
   419→* {axo_sequence} operations expect
   420→{xref_MCM}
   421→`mcm_form_section` with nested steps
   422→* {axj_structure} definitions expect
   423→{xref_MCM}
   424→`mcm_form_section`
   425→* {axj_field} definitions expect
   426→{xref_MCM}
   427→`mcm_form_bullet` under parent structure
   428→* {axk_premise} definitions expect
   429→{xref_MCM}
   430→`mcm_form_deflist`
   431→* {axs_*} section terms expect
   432→{xref_MCM}
   433→`mcm_form_section` for section headers
   434→* Most other categories expect
   435→{xref_MCM}
   436→`mcm_form_deflist`
   437→
   438→These expectations support the
   439→{axl_annotation}
   440→pattern where documents include `// ⟦axl_voices motif ...⟧` markers that are validated when AXL is in context.
   441→
   442→== Axial Control Terms
   443→
   444→[[axc_variable]]
   445→{axc_variable}::
   446→A
   447→{axl_motif}
   448→representing a temporary value holder denoted by distinctive delimiters that exists for the duration of an operational sequence.
   449→Implementation varies by context: shell variables in bash, dictionary entries in Python, sessionStorage in browser JavaScript.
   450→
   451→[[axc_fatal]]
   452→{axc_fatal}::
   453→A
   454→{axl_motif}
   455→representing immediate termination of the operational sequence with error status, preventing all subsequent steps from executing.
   456→Requires operator intervention or error recovery before retry.
   457→
   458→[[axc_warn]]
   459→{axc_warn}::
   460→A
   461→{axl_motif}
   462→representing display of a non-blocking warning message while allowing the operational sequence to continue execution.
   463→Alerts operators to concerning conditions without halting progress.
   464→
   465→[[axc_store]]
   466→{axc_store}::
   467→A
   468→{axl_motif}
   469→representing capture of a value from an operation result or computation into a
   470→{axc_variable}
   471→for later reference.
   472→Context determines storage mechanism while preserving semantic intent.
   473→
   474→[[axc_use]]
   475→{axc_use}::
   476→A
   477→{axl_motif}
   478→representing reference to a previously stored
   479→{axc_variable}
   480→value in the current operation.
   481→Retrieves stored values from context-appropriate storage location.
   482→
   483→[[axc_require]]
   484→{axc_require}::
   485→A
   486→{axl_motif}
   487→representing validation check that confirms an expected condition, triggering
   488→{axc_fatal}
   489→or alternate execution path if not met.
   490→Enforces preconditions and invariants throughout execution.
   491→
   492→[[axc_poll]]
   493→{axc_poll}::
   494→A
   495→{axl_motif}
   496→representing repeated execution of a status check at defined intervals until completion condition or timeout.
   497→Implements retry logic with configurable intervals and limits.
   498→
   499→[[axc_call]]
   500→{axc_call}::
   501→A
   502→{axl_motif}
   503→representing direct invocation of an external service, API endpoint, or command.
   504→Captures both success and error responses for processing.
   505→
   506→[[axc_submit]]
   507→{axc_submit}::
   508→A
   509→{axl_motif}
   510→representing initiation of an asynchronous operation that returns immediately with a handle for later completion tracking.
   511→Distinct from
   512→{axc_call}
   513→which expects synchronous response.
   514→
   515→[[axc_await]]
   516→{axc_await}::
   517→A
   518→{axl_motif}
   519→representing blocking wait on a submitted asynchronous operation until completion.
   520→Polls operation status until done, then returns final result or error.
   521→
   522→[[axc_show]]
   523→{axc_show}::
   524→A
   525→{axl_motif}
   526→representing presentation of operational facts or values to the operator.
   527→Implementation varies from console output to UI display based on context.
   528→
   529→[[axc_step]]
   530→{axc_step}::
   531→A
   532→{axl_motif}
   533→representing a normal progress message indicating successful completion of a processing stage.
   534→Provides operational visibility without implying warnings or errors.
   535→
   536→== Axial Environment Terms
   537→
   538→{axe_environment_s} specify execution contexts for control operations.
   539→{axl_voicing_s} of
   540→{axc_call},
   541→{axc_warn},
   542→{axc_fatal}
   543→and similar control motifs should include an appropriate
   544→{axe_environment}
   545→as a dimension to clarify how the control term manifests in that context.
   546→
   547→[[axe_environment]]
   548→{axe_environment}::
   549→A
   550→{axl_motif}
   551→representing an execution context that determines how control terms manifest.
   552→{axe_environment_s} appear as dimensions in
   553→{axl_voicing_s}
   554→to bind abstract control semantics to concrete operational behavior.
   555→
   556→[[axe_bash_interactive]]
   557→{axe_bash_interactive}::
   558→A
   559→{axl_motif}
   560→representing a bash shell session with human operator present at an interactive terminal.
   561→Feedback via stdout/stderr is immediate.
   562→Operator can interrupt (Ctrl-C) or respond to prompts.
   563→Warnings display as yellow text; fatals exit with non-zero status.
   564→
   565→[[axe_bash_unattended]]
   566→{axe_bash_unattended}::
   567→A
   568→{axl_motif}
   569→representing a bash script executing without human presence.
   570→Output captured to logs.
   571→No interactive prompts possible.
   572→Failures must be self-documenting for later analysis.
   573→
   574→[[axe_bash_scripted]]
   575→{axe_bash_scripted}::
   576→A
   577→{axl_motif}
   578→representing a bash script deliberately invoked to run to completion.
   579→No human interaction during execution.
   580→Succeeds atomically or fails with error status.
   581→Output may go to stdout/stderr for capture or display.
   582→Distinct from
   583→{axe_bash_unattended}
   584→which implies background/scheduled execution with log capture.
   585→
   586→[[axe_human_guide]]
   587→{axe_human_guide}::
   588→A
   589→{axl_motif}
   590→representing a human executing a specific rendered procedure document while interacting with an external system (web console, GUI application).
   591→The procedure document presents numbered steps; the human performs each in the external system.
   592→Feedback is visual confirmation in the external UI.
   593→Human judges success or failure of each step.
   594→Warnings are cautionary notes; fatals mean abort procedure.
   595→
   596→[[axe_rest_api]]
   597→{axe_rest_api}::
   598→A
   599→{axl_motif}
   600→representing invocation of an HTTP REST API endpoint.
   601→Request/response via JSON or other structured format.
   602→Authentication via bearer tokens, API keys, or similar.
   603→Calls return HTTP status codes and response bodies.
   604→
   605→[[axe_daemon_runtime]]
   606→{axe_daemon_runtime}::
   607→A
   608→{axl_motif}
   609→representing a long-running system process.
   610→Errors are logged, not displayed.
   611→Fatal means graceful shutdown with cleanup.
   612→No human in immediate feedback loop.
   613→
   614→== Axial Section Terms
   615→
   616→{axs_*} terms represent documentation sections for
   617→{axo_procedure}
   618→specifications.
   619→Each section motif captures a standard aspect of procedure documentation.
   620→{axl_voicing_s}
   621→adapt these abstract sections to interface-specific forms.
   622→
   623→[[axs_inputs]]
   624→{axs_inputs}::
   625→A
   626→{axl_motif}
   627→representing a section documenting what an
   628→{axo_procedure}
   629→requires to execute.
   630→Covers arguments, parameters, configuration, and input data.
   631→{axl_voicing_s}
   632→specify interface-appropriate input documentation:
   633→{axa_argument_list}
   634→for CLI,
   635→{axi_request_body}
   636→for REST APIs.
   637→
   638→[[axs_preconditions]]
   639→{axs_preconditions}::
   640→A
   641→{axl_motif}
   642→representing a section documenting environmental state requirements before
   643→{axo_procedure}
   644→execution.
   645→Optional section for procedures with significant prerequisites.
   646→Covers required services, authentication state, file system conditions, or resource availability.
   647→Distinct from
   648→{axs_inputs}
   649→which documents explicit parameters.
   650→
   651→[[axs_behavior]]
   652→{axs_behavior}::
   653→A
   654→{axl_motif}
   655→representing a section documenting what an
   656→{axo_procedure}
   657→does during execution.
   658→Describes the steps, actions, or transformations performed.
   659→{axl_voicing_s}
   660→may use
   661→{axo_sequence}
   662→patterns for ordered steps or prose for atomic operations.
   663→
   664→[[axs_outputs]]
   665→{axs_outputs}::
   666→A
   667→{axl_motif}
   668→representing a section documenting what an
   669→{axo_procedure}
   670→produces upon completion.
   671→Covers return values, created artifacts, modified state, or emitted data.
   672→{axl_voicing_s}
   673→specify interface-appropriate output documentation:
   674→{axi_response_body}
   675→for REST APIs, stdout/stderr patterns for CLI.
   676→
   677→[[axs_postconditions]]
   678→{axs_postconditions}::
   679→A
   680→{axl_motif}
   681→representing a section documenting environmental state guarantees after successful
   682→{axo_procedure}
   683→execution.
   684→Optional section for procedures with significant side effects.
   685→Covers created resources, modified permissions, or system state changes.
   686→Distinct from
   687→{axs_outputs}
   688→which documents explicit return values.
   689→
   690→[[axs_completion]]
   691→{axs_completion}::
   692→A
   693→{axl_motif}
   694→representing a section documenting how an
   695→{axo_procedure}
   696→signals success or failure.
   697→Covers exit codes, return status, completion indicators, or success criteria.
   698→{axl_voicing_s}
   699→specify interface-appropriate completion documentation:
   700→{axa_exit_uniform}
   701→or
   702→{axa_exit_enumerated}
   703→for CLI,
   704→HTTP status codes for REST APIs.
   705→
   706→[[axs_errors]]
   707→{axs_errors}::
   708→A
   709→{axl_motif}
   710→representing a section documenting failure modes of an
   711→{axo_procedure}.
   712→Optional section for procedures with non-obvious failure conditions.
   713→Covers error categories, recovery actions, or diagnostic guidance.
   714→Distinct from
   715→{axs_completion}
   716→which documents the signaling mechanism rather than failure semantics.
   717→
   718→=== Lifecycle Section Motifs
   719→
   720→These
   721→{axs_*}
   722→sections apply to specific lifecycle dimensions.
   723→
   724→[[axs_startup]]
   725→{axs_startup}::
   726→A
   727→{axl_motif}
   728→representing a section documenting initialization behavior for an
   729→{axd_longrunning}
   730→{axo_procedure}.
   731→Covers startup sequence, readiness criteria, and initialization dependencies.
   732→Required for
   733→{axd_longrunning}
   734→procedures.
   735→
   736→[[axs_shutdown]]
   737→{axs_shutdown}::
   738→A
   739→{axl_motif}
   740→representing a section documenting graceful termination for an
   741→{axd_longrunning}
   742→{axo_procedure}.
   743→Covers cleanup behavior, in-flight request handling, and resource release.
   744→Required for
   745→{axd_longrunning}
   746→procedures.
   747→
   748→[[axs_health]]
   749→{axs_health}::
   750→A
   751→{axl_motif}
   752→representing a section documenting health check semantics for an
   753→{axd_longrunning}
   754→{axo_procedure}.
   755→Covers liveness probes (is it running?), readiness probes (can it serve?), and health endpoints.
   756→Optional for
   757→{axd_longrunning}
   758→procedures.
   759→
   760→[[axs_signals]]
   761→{axs_signals}::
   762→A
   763→{axl_motif}
   764→representing a section documenting Unix signal handling for an
   765→{axo_procedure}.
   766→Covers SIGTERM (graceful stop), SIGHUP (reload config), SIGUSR1/2 (custom actions).
   767→Optional; most relevant for
   768→{axd_longrunning}
   769→but available to any
   770→{axo_command}.
   771→
   772→[[axs_recovery]]
   773→{axs_recovery}::
   774→A
   775→{axl_motif}
   776→representing a section documenting crash recovery behavior for an
   777→{axd_longrunning}
   778→{axo_procedure}.
   779→Covers restart behavior, state recovery, and failure mode handling.
   780→Optional for
   781→{axd_longrunning}
   782→procedures.
   783→
   784→[[axs_schedule]]
   785→{axs_schedule}::
   786→A
   787→{axl_motif}
   788→representing a section documenting timing specification for an
   789→{axd_periodic}
   790→{axo_procedure}.
   791→Covers cron expressions, intervals, or calendar-based triggers.
   792→Required for
   793→{axd_periodic}
   794→procedures.
   795→
   796→[[axs_idempotency]]
   797→{axs_idempotency}::
   798→A
   799→{axl_motif}
   800→representing a section documenting re-run safety for an
   801→{axd_periodic}
   802→{axo_procedure}.
   803→Covers duplicate handling, safe re-execution, and state management across runs.
   804→Optional for
   805→{axd_periodic}
   806→procedures.
   807→
   808→[[axs_overlap]]
   809→{axs_overlap}::
   810→A
   811→{axl_motif}
   812→representing a section documenting concurrent execution behavior for an
   813→{axd_periodic}
   814→{axo_procedure}.
   815→Covers what happens when a new run triggers while previous run is still executing.
   816→Options include: skip, queue, terminate previous, or allow parallel.
   817→Optional for
   818→{axd_periodic}
   819→procedures.
   820→
   821→== Axial Operation Terms
   822→
   823→=== Procedure Hierarchy
   824→
   825→{axo_procedure}
   826→is the superset
   827→{axl_motif}
   828→for procedural articulations.
   829→{axo_command},
   830→{axo_guide},
   831→and
   832→{axo_routine}
   833→are specialized forms that voice
   834→{axo_procedure}
   835→with additional constraints.
   836→
   837→{axo_sequence}
   838→is a structural characteristic describing HOW behavior is organized, not a procedure type.
   839→Most procedures ARE sequential.
   840→
   841→The following
   842→{axo_*}
   843→terms are NOT procedures and remain outside the hierarchy:
   844→{axo_entity},
   845→{axo_role},
   846→{axo_identity},
   847→{axo_actor},
   848→{axo_dependency}.
   849→
   850→[[axo_procedure]]
   851→{axo_procedure}::
   852→A
   853→{axl_motif}
   854→representing an executable operation with defined inputs, behavior, outputs, and completion semantics.
   855→{axo_procedure}
   856→is the superset for all procedural articulations.
   857→{axl_voicing_s}
   858→specialize by executor (machine vs human), reusability (terminal vs composite), and interface (CLI, REST, console).
   859→Documentation follows
   860→{axs_*}
   861→section patterns.
   862→
   863→[[axo_command]]
   864→{axo_command}::
   865→A
   866→{axl_motif}
   867→representing an
   868→{axo_procedure}
   869→executed by machine without human intervention.
   870→{axl_voices}
   871→{axo_procedure}
   872→with machine executor constraint.
   873→Requires
   874→{axe_environment}
   875→dimension specifying execution context.
   876→
   877→[[axo_guide]]
   878→{axo_guide}::
   879→A
   880→{axl_motif}
   881→representing an
   882→{axo_procedure}
   883→executed by a human operator following system-presented steps.
   884→{axl_voices}
   885→{axo_procedure}
   886→with human executor constraint.
   887→The system displays steps; the human performs them in external systems or consoles.
   888→Behavior steps use
   889→{axe_human_guide}
   890→control voicings.
   891→
   892→[[axo_routine]]
   893→{axo_routine}::
   894→A
   895→{axl_motif}
   896→representing a reusable
   897→{axo_procedure}
   898→invoked as a subsequence within other procedures.
   899→{axl_voices}
   900→{axo_procedure}
   901→with composite reusability constraint.
   902→Encapsulates common logic for consistent reuse across specifications.
   903→
   904→[[axo_sequence]]
   905→{axo_sequence}::
   906→A
   907→{axl_motif}
   908→representing a structural characteristic: ordered processing steps executed in defined progression.
   909→NOT a procedure type—describes HOW behavior is organized, not WHAT kind of procedure something is.
   910→Most
   911→{axo_procedure}
   912→voicings ARE sequential.
   913→Use within
   914→{axs_behavior}
   915→sections to document step ordering.
   916→
   917→[[axo_role]]
   918→{axo_role}::
   919→A
   920→{axl_motif}
   921→representing an operational identity pattern that defines capabilities and permissions.
   922→Determines available operations and access boundaries.
   923→
   924→[[axo_identity]]
   925→{axo_identity}::
   926→A
   927→{axl_motif}
   928→representing the abstract concept of an operational identity.
   929→An entity capable of authentication and holding authorities within a system.
   930→{axo_identity} is the pattern;
   931→{axo_actor_s}
   932→are named instantiations with specific bindings.
   933→
   934→[[axo_actor]]
   935→{axo_actor}::
   936→A
   937→{axl_motif}
   938→representing a specific named identity with defined responsibilities and exclusive authority over an operational domain.
   939→{axo_actor_s} instantiate
   940→{axo_identity}
   941→with concrete bindings (human via OAuth, service via service account) and permissions.
   942→
   943→[[axo_entity]]
   944→{axo_entity}::
   945→A
   946→{axl_motif}
   947→representing an object with both structure and behavior.
   948→Unlike
   949→{axr_record}
   950→which captures pure data,
   951→{axo_entity}
   952→includes methods, builder patterns, or lifecycle operations.
   953→{axl_voicing_s}
   954→describe the entity's data members and its behavioral interface (construction, mutation, registration).
   955→Typical manifestations include Rust structs with `impl` blocks, builder-pattern APIs, or objects that participate in registration protocols.
   956→
   957→== Axial Type Terms
   958→
   959→[[axt_type]]
   960→{axt_type}::
   961→A
   962→{axl_motif}
   963→representing a data format constraint that defines valid values and structures.
   964→Enforces data validation and enables type-safe operations.
   965→
   966→[[axt_string]]
   967→{axt_string}::
   968→A
   969→{axl_motif}
   970→representing text data type with optional pattern validation.
   971→{axl_voicing_s} may address context-specific concerns including:
   972→+
   973→* Encoding (ASCII, UTF-8, locale-dependent character sets)
   974→* Termination semantics (null-terminated, length-prefixed, delimiter-bounded)
   975→* Mutability (immutable values, mutable buffers)
   976→* Memory management (owned, borrowed, reference-counted)
   977→* Safety constraints (shell-safe, URL-safe, JSON-safe, SQL-safe)
   978→* Format patterns (timestamps, identifiers, hashes, paths)
   979→* Size and bounds (fixed-length, maximum length, unbounded)
   980→
   981→[[axt_number]]
   982→{axt_number}::
   983→A
   984→{axl_motif}
   985→representing numeric value type supporting integers and floating point.
   986→May specify range constraints or precision requirements.
   987→
   988→[[axt_boolean]]
   989→{axt_boolean}::
   990→A
   991→{axl_motif}
   992→representing binary true/false value type.
   993→Maps to language-specific boolean implementations.
   994→
   995→[[axt_array]]
   996→{axt_array}::
   997→A
   998→{axl_motif}
   999→representing ordered collection of typed elements.
  1000→Specifies element type and optional size constraints.
  1001→
  1002→[[axt_object]]
  1003→{axt_object}::
  1004→A
  1005→{axl_motif}
  1006→representing named field collection forming a composite type.
  1007→Contains typed fields with defined cardinality.
  1008→
  1009→[[axt_enumeration]]
  1010→{axt_enumeration}::
  1011→A
  1012→{axl_motif}
  1013→representing constrained set of valid string values.
  1014→Restricts values to predefined options.
  1015→
  1016→[[axt_enum_value]]
  1017→{axt_enum_value}::
  1018→A
  1019→{axl_motif}
  1020→representing single allowed option within an
  1021→{axt_enumeration}.
  1022→Must be explicitly listed in enumeration definition.
  1023→
  1024→[[axt_variant]]
  1025→{axt_variant}::
  1026→A
  1027→{axl_motif}
  1028→representing a value that can take one of several distinct forms, where each form may have different structure.
  1029→Unlike
  1030→{axt_enumeration}
  1031→which constrains to predefined strings,
  1032→{axt_variant}
  1033→allows structurally different alternatives.
  1034→{axl_voicing_s} define the specific forms and discrimination logic for their context.
  1035→
  1036→== Axial Record Terms
  1037→
  1038→{axr_record_s} and
  1039→{axr_member_s}
  1040→provide format-agnostic patterns for structured data.
  1041→Format-specific variants (JSON, protobuf, XML, etc.) capture serialization-specific semantics.
  1042→{axr_member_s} inherit format context from their parent
  1043→{axr_record}.
  1044→
  1045→[[axr_record]]
  1046→{axr_record}::
  1047→A
  1048→{axl_motif}
  1049→representing a named collection of typed
  1050→{axr_member_s}.
  1051→Abstract pattern that format-specific variants voice with serialization semantics.
  1052→Use format-specific variants (
  1053→{axr_record_json},
  1054→{axr_record_protobuf},
  1055→etc.) when format is known.
  1056→
  1057→[[axr_record_json]]
  1058→{axr_record_json}::
  1059→A
  1060→{axl_motif}
  1061→representing a JSON object structure.
  1062→{axr_member_s} are unordered, string-keyed properties.
  1063→Requires
  1064→{axd_nullable}
  1065→specification since JSON has explicit null.
  1066→
  1067→[[axr_record_protobuf]]
  1068→{axr_record_protobuf}::
  1069→A
  1070→{axl_motif}
  1071→representing a protobuf message.
  1072→{axr_member_s} require
  1073→{axd_field_number}
  1074→and
  1075→{axd_wire_type}
  1076→dimensions.
  1077→Supports schema evolution through field numbering.
  1078→
  1079→[[axr_record_xml]]
  1080→{axr_record_xml}::
  1081→A
  1082→{axl_motif}
  1083→representing an XML element structure.
  1084→{axr_member_s} require
  1085→{axd_xml_form}
  1086→to distinguish attributes from child elements.
  1087→Order may be significant depending on schema.
  1088→
  1089→[[axr_record_yaml]]
  1090→{axr_record_yaml}::
  1091→A
  1092→{axl_motif}
  1093→representing a YAML mapping.
  1094→Superset of JSON with anchors, aliases, and multi-document support.
  1095→{axr_member_s} follow JSON-like semantics with extended syntax.
  1096→
  1097→[[axr_record_binary]]
  1098→{axr_record_binary}::
  1099→A
  1100→{axl_motif}
  1101→representing a binary/memory structure.
  1102→{axr_member_s} require
  1103→{axd_endianness}
  1104→for multi-byte fields.
  1105→Fixed layout with explicit size and alignment.
  1106→
  1107→[[axr_member]]
  1108→{axr_member}::
  1109→A
  1110→{axl_motif}
  1111→representing a named, typed element within an
  1112→{axr_record}.
  1113→Inherits serialization format from parent
  1114→{axr_record}.
  1115→Qualified by
  1116→{axd_dimension_s}
  1117→in
  1118→{axl_annotation}:
  1119→`// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_nullable⟧`
  1120→
  1121→== Axial JSON Terms
  1122→
  1123→[[axj_structure]]
  1124→{axj_structure}::
  1125→A
  1126→{axl_motif}
  1127→representing named schema definition for JSON objects.
  1128→Defines complete object shape with field specifications.
  1129→
  1130→[[axj_field]]
  1131→{axj_field}::
  1132→A
  1133→{axl_motif}
  1134→representing named, typed element within a
  1135→{axj_structure}.
  1136→Combines tag identifier with type constraint.
  1137→
  1138→[[axj_field_tag]]
  1139→{axj_field_tag}::
  1140→A
  1141→{axl_motif}
  1142→representing the key identifier for a
  1143→{axj_field}.
  1144→Follows naming conventions of target specification.
  1145→
  1146→[[axj_field_type]]
  1147→{axj_field_type}::
  1148→A
  1149→{axl_motif}
  1150→representing the value constraint for a
  1151→{axj_field}.
  1152→Determines parsing and validation behavior.
  1153→
  1154→[[axj_array_element]]
  1155→{axj_array_element}::
  1156→A
  1157→{axl_motif}
  1158→representing type constraint for members of an
  1159→{axt_array}
  1160→field.
  1161→Applies uniformly to all array elements.
  1162→
  1163→[[axj_nested_field]]
  1164→{axj_nested_field}::
  1165→A
  1166→{axl_motif}
  1167→representing fields within object-typed fields enabling hierarchy.
  1168→Maintains type safety through nested validation.
  1169→
  1170→[[axj_root_structure]]
  1171→{axj_root_structure}::
  1172→A
  1173→{axl_motif}
  1174→representing top-level JSON object schema.
  1175→Entry point for JSON document validation.
  1176→
  1177→[[axj_field_cardinality]]
  1178→{axj_field_cardinality}::
  1179→A
  1180→{axl_motif}
  1181→representing required, optional, or repeated nature of a field.
  1182→Maps to validation rules in implementation.
  1183→
  1184→[[axj_type_reference]]
  1185→{axj_type_reference}::
  1186→A
  1187→{axl_motif}
  1188→representing pointer to another
  1189→{axj_structure}
  1190→for composition.
  1191→Maintains referential integrity across definitions.
  1192→
  1193→== Axial Argument Terms
  1194→
  1195→[[axa_argument]]
  1196→{axa_argument}::
  1197→A
  1198→{axl_motif}
  1199→representing command parameter accepting a value.
  1200→Foundation for CLI and function interfaces.
  1201→
  1202→[[axa_positional]]
  1203→{axa_positional}::
  1204→A
  1205→{axl_motif}
  1206→representing order-determines-meaning parameter in command invocation.
  1207→Position in argument list defines semantic role.
  1208→
  1209→[[axa_keyword]]
  1210→{axa_keyword}::
  1211→A
  1212→{axl_motif}
  1213→representing name-determines-meaning parameter with explicit identifier.
  1214→Order-independent with explicit naming.
  1215→
  1216→[[axa_long_option]]
  1217→{axa_long_option}::
  1218→A
  1219→{axl_motif}
  1220→representing keyword argument with `--` prefix following GNU conventions.
  1221→Uses hyphenated multi-word names.
  1222→
  1223→[[axa_short_option]]
  1224→{axa_short_option}::
  1225→A
  1226→{axl_motif}
  1227→representing keyword argument with single `-` prefix for brevity.
  1228→Often provides shorthand for
  1229→{axa_long_option}.
  1230→
  1231→[[axa_option_name]]
  1232→{axa_option_name}::
  1233→A
  1234→{axl_motif}
  1235→representing the identifier portion of a keyword argument.
  1236→Defines the semantic meaning of the option.
  1237→
  1238→[[axa_option_value]]
  1239→{axa_option_value}::
  1240→A
  1241→{axl_motif}
  1242→representing the data portion following an option identifier.
  1243→Subject to
  1244→{axa_argument_type}
  1245→constraints.
  1246→
  1247→[[axa_argument_type]]
  1248→{axa_argument_type}::
  1249→A
  1250→{axl_motif}
  1251→representing expected data format for an
  1252→{axa_argument}.
  1253→Enforces type safety at interface boundary.
  1254→
  1255→[[axa_required]]
  1256→{axa_required}::
  1257→A
  1258→{axl_motif}
  1259→representing attribute indicating an
  1260→{axa_argument}
  1261→must be provided.
  1262→Absence triggers
  1263→{axc_fatal}
  1264→or error response.
  1265→
  1266→[[axa_optional]]
  1267→{axa_optional}::
  1268→A
  1269→{axl_motif}
  1270→representing attribute indicating an
  1271→{axa_argument}
  1272→may be omitted.
  1273→Provides flexibility in interface usage.
  1274→
  1275→[[axa_defaulted]]
  1276→{axa_defaulted}::
  1277→A
  1278→{axl_motif}
  1279→representing attribute indicating an
  1280→{axa_argument}
  1281→has a fallback value.
  1282→Specifies behavior when
  1283→{axa_optional}
  1284→argument omitted.
  1285→
  1286→[[axa_argument_list]]
  1287→{axa_argument_list}::
  1288→A
  1289→{axl_motif}
  1290→representing a structured section documenting an operation's arguments.
  1291→{axl_voices}
  1292→{axs_inputs}
  1293→in CLI context.
  1294→Within an
  1295→{axa_argument_list},
  1296→{axd_dimension}
  1297→annotations may precede bullet items to qualify argument usage without requiring anchors.
  1298→Each item references a globally-defined argument term.
  1299→The voicing is placed on the section header term definition, not repeated at each usage.
  1300→+
  1301→Example section header definition:
  1302→+
  1303→[source,asciidoc]
  1304→----
  1305→[[jjds_arguments]]
  1306→// ⟦axl_voices axa_argument_list⟧
  1307→{jjds_arguments}::
  1308→Section header introducing operation arguments.
  1309→----
  1310→+
  1311→Example usage in an operation:
  1312→+
  1313→[source,asciidoc]
  1314→----
  1315→{jjds_arguments}
  1316→
  1317→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1318→* {jjda_silks}
  1319→
  1320→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1321→* {jjda_file}
  1322→----
  1323→
  1324→[[axa_cli_option]]
  1325→{axa_cli_option}::
  1326→A
  1327→{axl_motif}
  1328→representing a command-line option definition.
  1329→{axl_voicing_s}
  1330→require the definition text to specify:
  1331→+
  1332→* The long form (e.g., `--file`)
  1333→* The short form, or its absence (e.g., `-f` or "none")
  1334→+
  1335→Example:
  1336→+
  1337→[source,asciidoc]
  1338→----
  1339→[[jjda_file]]
  1340→// ⟦axl_voices axa_cli_option⟧
  1341→{jjda_file}::
  1342→Path to the Gallops JSON file.
  1343→Long: `--file`.
  1344→Short: none.
  1345→Default: `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.
  1346→----
  1347→
  1348→[[axa_exit_uniform]]
  1349→{axa_exit_uniform}::
  1350→A
  1351→{axl_motif}
  1352→representing an Exit Status section that follows standard Unix conventions.
  1353→{axl_voices}
  1354→{axs_completion}
  1355→in CLI context.
  1356→Exit 0 indicates success; any non-zero exit indicates error.
  1357→{axl_voicing_s}
  1358→may use terse notation since the pattern is conventional.
  1359→The voicing is placed on the section header term definition, not repeated at each usage.
  1360→Specifications define separate section header terms for uniform vs enumerated exit patterns.
  1361→+
  1362→Example section header definition:
  1363→+
  1364→[source,asciidoc]
  1365→----
  1366→[[jjds_exit_uniform]]
  1367→// ⟦axl_voices axa_exit_uniform⟧
  1368→{jjds_exit_uniform}::
  1369→Section header for operations with standard exit semantics.
  1370→----
  1371→+
  1372→Example usage:
  1373→+
  1374→[source,asciidoc]
  1375→----
  1376→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1377→----
  1378→
  1379→[[axa_exit_enumerated]]
  1380→{axa_exit_enumerated}::
  1381→A
  1382→{axl_motif}
  1383→representing an Exit Status section with distinct exit codes carrying specific meanings.
  1384→{axl_voices}
  1385→{axs_completion}
  1386→in CLI context.
  1387→{axl_voicing_s}
  1388→require the definition text to list each exit code and its meaning.
  1389→The voicing is placed on the section header term definition, not repeated at each usage.
  1390→Specifications define separate section header terms for uniform vs enumerated exit patterns.
  1391→+
  1392→Example section header definition:
  1393→+
  1394→[source,asciidoc]
  1395→----
  1396→[[jjds_exit_enumerated]]
  1397→// ⟦axl_voices axa_exit_enumerated⟧
  1398→{jjds_exit_enumerated}::
  1399→Section header for operations with distinct exit codes.
  1400→----
  1401→+
  1402→Example usage:
  1403→+
  1404→[source,asciidoc]
  1405→----
  1406→{jjds_exit_enumerated}
  1407→
  1408→* 0 — found and valid
  1409→* 1 — not found
  1410→* 2 — found but malformed
  1411→----
  1412→
  1413→== Axial Interface Terms
  1414→
  1415→[[axi_command_interface]]
  1416→{axi_command_interface}::
  1417→A
  1418→{axl_motif}
  1419→representing complete specification of a command's invocation pattern.
  1420→Defines syntax and semantics for command usage.
  1421→
  1422→[[axi_subcommand]]
  1423→{axi_subcommand}::
  1424→A
  1425→{axl_motif}
  1426→representing nested command within parent command structure.
  1427→Inherits context from parent command.
  1428→
  1429→[[axi_command_signature]]
  1430→{axi_command_signature}::
  1431→A
  1432→{axl_motif}
  1433→representing complete parameter specification for a command.
  1434→Serves as contract for command invocation.
  1435→
  1436→[[axi_endpoint]]
  1437→{axi_endpoint}::
  1438→A
  1439→{axl_motif}
  1440→representing URL path accepting HTTP requests.
  1441→Defines resource location and accepted operations.
  1442→
  1443→[[axi_api_method]]
  1444→{axi_api_method}::
  1445→A
  1446→{axl_motif}
  1447→representing HTTP verb combined with
  1448→{axi_endpoint}
  1449→specification.
  1450→Includes request and response specifications.
  1451→
  1452→[[axi_url_parameter]]
  1453→{axi_url_parameter}::
  1454→A
  1455→{axl_motif}
  1456→representing query string key-value pair in URL.
  1457→Appears after `?` in URL structure.
  1458→
  1459→[[axi_hash_parameter]]
  1460→{axi_hash_parameter}::
  1461→A
  1462→{axl_motif}
  1463→representing fragment identifier for client-side state.
  1464→Persists state without server round-trips.
  1465→
  1466→[[axi_request_body]]
  1467→{axi_request_body}::
  1468→A
  1469→{axl_motif}
  1470→representing structured input payload for HTTP requests.
  1471→Validated against
  1472→{axj_structure}
  1473→definitions.
  1474→
  1475→[[axi_response_body]]
  1476→{axi_response_body}::
  1477→A
  1478→{axl_motif}
  1479→representing structured output payload from HTTP responses.
  1480→Conforms to documented
  1481→{axj_structure}.
  1482→
  1483→[[axi_cli_interface]]
  1484→{axi_cli_interface}::
  1485→A
  1486→{axl_motif}
  1487→representing command-line invocation pattern specification.
  1488→Documents
  1489→{axa_argument_s}
  1490→and behavior.
  1491→
  1492→[[axi_cli_program]]
  1493→{axi_cli_program}::
  1494→A
  1495→{axl_motif}
  1496→representing a Unix-style command-line program.
  1497→Invoked from a shell with arguments.
  1498→Writes results to stdout, errors to stderr.
  1499→Returns exit status: 0 for success, non-zero for failure.
  1500→Suitable for scripted, non-interactive use.
  1501→
  1502→[[axi_cli_command_group]]
  1503→{axi_cli_command_group}::
  1504→A
  1505→{axl_motif}
  1506→representing a namespace within a CLI that contains subcommands.
  1507→Does not perform operations itself; serves as organizational container.
  1508→May be nested within an
  1509→{axi_cli_program}
  1510→or another
  1511→{axi_cli_command_group}.
  1512→Inherits Unix CLI semantics from parent.
  1513→
  1514→[[axi_cli_subcommand]]
  1515→{axi_cli_subcommand}::
  1516→A
  1517→{axl_motif}
  1518→representing a leaf operation within an
  1519→{axi_cli_program}
  1520→or
  1521→{axi_cli_command_group}.
  1522→Performs an actual operation; not a container for other commands.
  1523→Inherits Unix CLI semantics from parent: arguments, stdout, stderr, exit status.
  1524→
  1525→[[axi_rest_api]]
  1526→{axi_rest_api}::
  1527→A
  1528→{axl_motif}
  1529→representing resource-based web service interface.
  1530→Maps operations to HTTP methods and resources.
  1531→
  1532→[[axi_rpc_interface]]
  1533→{axi_rpc_interface}::
  1534→A
  1535→{axl_motif}
  1536→representing remote procedure call pattern for service invocation.
  1537→Emphasizes operation semantics over HTTP methods.
  1538→
  1539→[[axi_sse_endpoint]]
  1540→{axi_sse_endpoint}::
  1541→A
  1542→{axl_motif}
  1543→representing server-sent event stream for push notifications.
  1544→Enables real-time updates to connected clients.
  1545→
  1546→[[axi_cc_slash_command]]
  1547→{axi_cc_slash_command}::
  1548→A
  1549→{axl_motif}
  1550→representing a Claude Code slash command skill artifact.
  1551→Implemented as a `.claude/commands/*.md` file containing orchestration logic that the LLM executes as a skill.
  1552→Slash commands coordinate multiple
  1553→{axi_cli_subcommand}
  1554→invocations, gather context, and apply workflow policies.
  1555→
  1556→[[axi_cc_claudemd_verb]]
  1557→{axi_cc_claudemd_verb}::
  1558→A
  1559→{axl_motif}
  1560→representing an action verb defined in a
  1561→{axi_cc_claudemd_section}
  1562→that maps user intent to tool-layer operations.
  1563→The verb exists as a concept in the LLM context — either mapping directly to a single
  1564→{axi_cli_subcommand}
  1565→(direct verb) or orchestrating multiple operations (multistep verb implemented by a
  1566→{axi_cc_slash_command}).
  1567→
  1568→[[axi_cc_claudemd_section]]
  1569→{axi_cc_claudemd_section}::
  1570→A
  1571→{axl_motif}
  1572→representing a managed section within a project CLAUDE.md file.
  1573→Provides persistent LLM context that bridges specification-layer concepts to runtime execution.
  1574→Typically contains command references, vocabulary mappings, and operational guidance.
  1575→
  1576→== Axial Dimension Terms
  1577→
  1578→{axd_dimension_s} are modifiers that appear in
  1579→{axl_annotation_s}
  1580→to specify characteristics of members and arguments.
  1581→Unlike primary
  1582→{axl_motif_s},
  1583→{axd_dimension_s}
  1584→do not stand alone but qualify how a term voices its primary motif.
  1585→Applicability of
  1586→{axd_dimension_s}
  1587→depends on serialization format context.
  1588→
  1589→[[axd_dimension]]
  1590→{axd_dimension}::
  1591→A characteristic modifier that qualifies a member or argument in an
  1592→{axl_annotation}.
  1593→{axd_dimension_s} appear after the primary
  1594→{axl_motif}
  1595→in annotation format: `// ⟦axl_voices primary dimension ...⟧`
  1596→
  1597→=== Value Dimensions
  1598→
  1599→These
  1600→{axd_dimension_s}
  1601→describe value characteristics and apply across most serialization formats.
  1602→They encompass both presence semantics (required, optional, defaulted) and structural semantics (repeated, list).
  1603→
  1604→[[axd_required]]
  1605→{axd_required}::
  1606→Member or argument must be present.
  1607→Absence triggers validation error or
  1608→{axc_fatal}.
  1609→
  1610→[[axd_optional]]
  1611→{axd_optional}::
  1612→Member or argument may be omitted.
  1613→Absence is valid and handled gracefully.
  1614→
  1615→[[axd_nullable]]
  1616→{axd_nullable}::
  1617→Null, nil, or None is a valid value, distinct from absent.
  1618→Required for JSON where null is an explicit value type.
  1619→Some formats (protobuf) conflate nullable with optional.
  1620→
  1621→[[axd_none]]
  1622→{axd_none}::
  1623→Section or field is explicitly declared as having no content.
  1624→Distinguishes intentional absence from omission.
  1625→Use in section annotations to mark compliance with required sections that have no applicable content.
  1626→
  1627→[[axd_defaulted]]
  1628→{axd_defaulted}::
  1629→Has fallback value when absent.
  1630→Voicing should specify the default value in prose.
  1631→
  1632→[[axd_repeated]]
  1633→{axd_repeated}::
  1634→May have multiple values (array, list, repeated field).
  1635→Absence typically means empty collection, not error.
  1636→Applies to usage sites (arguments, members) where multiple occurrences are permitted.
  1637→
  1638→[[axd_list]]
  1639→{axd_list}::
  1640→Single value containing delimited multiple values.
  1641→Distinct from
  1642→{axd_repeated}
  1643→which indicates multiple separate occurrences.
  1644→Applies to type voicings where the type intrinsically represents a delimited collection.
  1645→Voicing should specify the delimiter in prose (space-delimited, comma-delimited, etc.).
  1646→
  1647→=== Format-Specific Dimensions
  1648→
  1649→These
  1650→{axd_dimension_s}
  1651→apply only to specific serialization formats.
  1652→Using them in forbidden contexts is a type-scheme error.
  1653→
  1654→[[axd_field_number]]
  1655→{axd_field_number}::
  1656→Numeric field identifier for protobuf encoding.
  1657→Required for protobuf format.
  1658→Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, binary.
  1659→
  1660→[[axd_wire_type]]
  1661→{axd_wire_type}::
  1662→Protobuf encoding type (varint, fixed32, fixed64, length-delimited).
  1663→Required for protobuf format.
  1664→Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, binary.
  1665→
  1666→[[axd_endianness]]
  1667→{axd_endianness}::
  1668→Byte order for binary encoding (big-endian, little-endian).
  1669→Required for binary format.
  1670→Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, protobuf.
  1671→
  1672→[[axd_xml_form]]
  1673→{axd_xml_form}::
  1674→Whether XML member is attribute or child element.
  1675→Required for XML format.
  1676→Forbidden for JSON, YAML, protobuf, binary.
  1677→
  1678→=== Lifecycle Dimensions
  1679→
  1680→These
  1681→{axd_dimension_s}
  1682→qualify
  1683→{axo_procedure}
  1684→voicings to specify execution lifecycle.
  1685→Exactly one lifecycle dimension is required for any
  1686→{axo_procedure}
  1687→voicing.
  1688→Lifecycle dimensions are mutually exclusive.
  1689→
  1690→[[axd_transient]]
  1691→{axd_transient}::
  1692→{axo_procedure}
  1693→executes and completes.
  1694→The default lifecycle for most procedures.
  1695→{axs_completion}
  1696→documents exit status or return value.
  1697→
  1698→[[axd_longrunning]]
  1699→{axd_longrunning}::
  1700→{axo_procedure}
  1701→runs continuously until explicitly stopped.
  1702→{axs_completion}
  1703→documents startup success criteria, not termination.
  1704→Requires
  1705→{axs_startup}
  1706→and
  1707→{axs_shutdown}
  1708→sections.
  1709→May include
  1710→{axs_health},
  1711→{axs_signals},
  1712→{axs_recovery}
  1713→sections.
  1714→
  1715→[[axd_periodic]]
  1716→{axd_periodic}::
  1717→{axo_procedure}
  1718→executes on a schedule.
  1719→{axs_completion}
  1720→applies per-run.
  1721→Requires
  1722→{axs_schedule}
  1723→section.
  1724→May include
  1725→{axs_idempotency},
  1726→{axs_overlap}
  1727→sections.
  1728→
  1729→=== Regime Dimensions
  1730→
  1731→These
  1732→{axd_dimension_s}
  1733→qualify regime structure annotations.
  1734→
  1735→[[axd_conditional]]
  1736→{axd_conditional}::
  1737→Group activation depends on one or more gate conditions.
  1738→A group annotated with
  1739→{axd_conditional}
  1740→requires corresponding hierarchy markers in the subdocument to include at least one
  1741→{axhrgc_gate}
  1742→specifying the activation criteria.
  1743→
  1744→=== Dimension Applicability
  1745→
  1746→Per-format guidance for
  1747→{axd_dimension}
  1748→usage:
  1749→
  1750→[cols="<,^,^,^,^,^",options="header"]
  1751→|===
  1752→|Dimension |JSON |Protobuf |XML |Binary |YAML
  1753→
  1754→|{axd_required}     |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend
  1755→|{axd_optional}     |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend
  1756→|{axd_nullable}     |required  |implicit  |recommend |context   |recommend
  1757→|{axd_defaulted}    |optional  |required  |optional  |optional  |optional
  1758→|{axd_repeated}     |recommend |required  |recommend |recommend |recommend
  1759→|{axd_list}         |optional  |optional  |optional  |optional  |optional
  1760→|{axd_field_number} |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden
  1761→|{axd_wire_type}    |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden
  1762→|{axd_endianness}   |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden |required  |forbidden
  1763→|{axd_xml_form}     |forbidden |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden
  1764→|===
  1765→
  1766→Legend: required = must specify; recommend = should specify; optional = may specify; implicit = format handles; context = depends on usage; forbidden = not applicable.
  1767→
  1768→== Axial Format Terms
  1769→
  1770→{axf_format_s} specify how configuration data is serialized and carried.
  1771→{axrg_assignment_s} use a particular
  1772→{axf_format}
  1773→to encode
  1774→{axrg_variable}
  1775→values in files that can be sourced, included, or parsed.
  1776→
  1777→[[axf_format]]
  1778→{axf_format}::
  1779→A
  1780→{axl_motif}
  1781→representing a serialization encoding for configuration data.
  1782→{axf_format_s} determine how
  1783→{axrg_assignment_s}
  1784→are written to and read from files.
  1785→
  1786→[[axf_bash]]
  1787→{axf_bash}::
  1788→A
  1789→{axl_motif}
  1790→representing bash script format for configuration.
  1791→Files are source-able via `. file` or `source file`.
  1792→Variables declared as `VAR=value` without export, or `export VAR=value` for environment propagation.
  1793→
  1794→[[axf_makefile]]
  1795→{axf_makefile}::
  1796→A
  1797→{axl_motif}
  1798→representing makefile format for configuration.
  1799→Files are includable via `include file`.
  1800→Variables declared as `VAR := value` for immediate expansion or `VAR = value` for deferred expansion.
  1801→
  1802→[[axf_json]]
  1803→{axf_json}::
  1804→A
  1805→{axl_motif}
  1806→representing JSON format for configuration.
  1807→Standard JSON object structure with string keys mapping to typed values.
  1808→Parsed by standard JSON libraries.
  1809→
  1810→[[axf_yaml]]
  1811→{axf_yaml}::
  1812→A
  1813→{axl_motif}
  1814→representing YAML format for configuration.
  1815→Superset of JSON with additional syntax for readability.
  1816→Parsed by YAML libraries with attention to version-specific semantics.
  1817→
  1818→== Axial Regime Terms
  1819→
  1820→{axrg_regime_s} are structured configuration systems that coordinate specification, validation, and rendering of typed configuration data.
  1821→
  1822→[[axrg_regime]]
  1823→{axrg_regime}::
  1824→A
  1825→{axl_motif}
  1826→representing a structured configuration system.
  1827→A
  1828→{axrg_regime}
  1829→binds related
  1830→{axrg_variable_s}
  1831→under a namespace prefix, with coordinated specification, validation, and rendering components.
  1832→The specification defines what can be configured;
  1833→{axrg_assignment_s}
  1834→provide specific values.
  1835→
  1836→[[axrg_variable]]
  1837→{axrg_variable}::
  1838→A
  1839→{axl_motif}
  1840→representing a named configuration element at schema level.
  1841→Each
  1842→{axrg_variable}
  1843→belongs to an
  1844→{axrg_regime}
  1845→and defines a name, type constraints, and relationships with other variables.
  1846→{axrg_variable_s} are the "what can be configured" definitions;
  1847→{axrg_assignment_s}
  1848→bind values to them.
  1849→
  1850→[[axrg_assignment]]
  1851→{axrg_assignment}::
  1852→A
  1853→{axl_motif}
  1854→representing a specific value bound to an
  1855→{axrg_variable}.
  1856→{axrg_assignment_s} are carried in files of a particular
  1857→{axf_format}
  1858→(bash, makefile, JSON, etc.).
  1859→Multiple assignment files may exist for the same
  1860→{axrg_regime},
  1861→each providing different configuration instances.
  1862→
  1863→[[axrg_prefix]]
  1864→{axrg_prefix}::
  1865→A
  1866→{axl_motif}
  1867→representing the stable character sequence that namespaces all
  1868→{axrg_variable_s}
  1869→within an
  1870→{axrg_regime}.
  1871→Typically composed of a project marker and regime marker (e.g., `RBRR_` = `RB` + `RR` + `_`).
  1872→Once established, an
  1873→{axrg_prefix}
  1874→becomes deeply embedded across code, configuration files, and documentation.
  1875→Changing an
  1876→{axrg_prefix}
  1877→requires coordinated refactoring across all consuming artifacts.
  1878→
  1879→== Axial Regime Annotation Terms
  1880→
  1881→Regime annotations use the prefix-discriminated form (`//ax...` with no space after `//`) to provide structured metadata about regime definitions in AsciiDoc specifications.
  1882→Two complementary annotation families enable regime specification across parent documents and their subdocuments.
  1883→
  1884→=== Definition-Site Voicing Annotations (axvr_*)
  1885→
  1886→{axvr_*} annotations appear in parent documents between anchor and definition, declaring the regime role of the term being defined.
  1887→The surface syntax follows the prefix-discriminated pattern:
  1888→
  1889→[source,asciidoc]
  1890→----
  1891→[[rbrv_sigil]]
  1892→//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_xname
  1893→{rbrv_sigil}::
  1894→Vessel identifier for build configurations.
  1895→----
  1896→
  1897→The annotation line starts with `//axvr_` (no space after `//`), followed by the specific voicing term, then space-separated dimension and type terms.
  1898→The anchor immediately preceding the annotation must match the first
  1899→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  1900→in the definition text that follows.
  1901→
  1902→[[axvr_regime]]
  1903→{axvr_regime}::
  1904→Voices a regime definition.
  1905→The annotated term defines a structured configuration system that binds related
  1906→{axrg_variable_s}
  1907→under a namespace prefix.
  1908→+
  1909→{axd_dimension_s}:
  1910→{axf_format}
  1911→(specifying how
  1912→{axrg_assignment_s}
  1913→are carried).
  1914→
  1915→[[axvr_variable]]
  1916→{axvr_variable}::
  1917→Voices a regime variable definition.
  1918→The annotated term defines a named configuration element within a regime.
  1919→The definition text must include, as its second
  1920→{mcm_attribute_reference},
  1921→the parent regime (an anchor that voices
  1922→{axrg_regime}).
  1923→This ties each variable unequivocally to its regime.
  1924→+
  1925→{axd_dimension_s}:
  1926→{axd_required}
  1927→or
  1928→{axd_optional},
  1929→type
  1930→{axl_motif}
  1931→(base type or type voicing).
  1932→
  1933→[[axvr_group]]
  1934→{axvr_group}::
  1935→Voices a regime group definition.
  1936→The annotated term defines a named grouping of related
  1937→{axrg_variable_s}
  1938→within a regime.
  1939→The definition text must include, as its second
  1940→{mcm_attribute_reference},
  1941→the parent regime (an anchor that voices
  1942→{axrg_regime}).
  1943→+
  1944→{axd_dimension_s}:
  1945→{axd_conditional}
  1946→(group activation depends on gate conditions in subdocument).
  1947→
  1948→=== Standalone Hierarchy Markers (axhr*_)
  1949→
  1950→Hierarchy markers appear in regime subdocuments as standalone annotations, NOT between anchor and definition.
  1951→Each marker uses the prefix-discriminated form and reads ahead a specified number of
  1952→{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
  1953→from the following text to establish hierarchical context.
  1954→
  1955→The surface syntax:
  1956→
  1957→[source,asciidoc]
  1958→----
  1959→//axhrb_regime
  1960→{rbrv_regime}
  1961→
  1962→//axhrgv_variable
  1963→{rbrv_sigil}
  1964→----
  1965→
  1966→The hierarchy marker line starts with `//axhr` (no space after `//`), followed by the sub-prefix identifying the marker type.
  1967→Following text contains the
  1968→{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
  1969→that the marker reads ahead.
  1970→
  1971→==== Marker Terms and Lookahead Arities
  1972→
  1973→[cols="<,^,<",options="header"]
  1974→|===
  1975→|Marker |Lookahead |Reads
  1976→
  1977→|{axhrb_regime}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |The regime this subdoc describes
  1978→|{axhrv_variable}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |An ungrouped regime variable
  1979→|{axhrgb_group}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A group (must match {axvr_group} in parent)
  1980→|{axhrgc_gate}      |2 {mcm_attribute_reference_s}|Enumerated variable + enum value
  1981→|{axhrgv_variable}  |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A variable within current group
  1982→|===
  1983→
  1984→[[axhrb_regime]]
  1985→{axhrb_regime}::
  1986→Marks the regime that a subdocument describes.
  1987→Lookahead: 1
  1988→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  1989→— the regime this subdoc describes.
  1990→The referenced attribute must correspond to an anchor annotated with
  1991→{axvr_regime}
  1992→in the parent document.
  1993→
  1994→[[axhrv_variable]]
  1995→{axhrv_variable}::
  1996→Marks an ungrouped regime variable in a subdocument.
  1997→Lookahead: 1
  1998→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  1999→— the variable being detailed.
  2000→
  2001→[[axhrgb_group]]
  2002→{axhrgb_group}::
  2003→Marks a group boundary within a subdocument.
  2004→Lookahead: 1
  2005→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  2006→— the group being detailed.
  2007→The referenced attribute must match an anchor annotated with
  2008→{axvr_group}
  2009→in the parent document.
  2010→
  2011→[[axhrgc_gate]]
  2012→{axhrgc_gate}::
  2013→Marks a conditional activation gate within a group.
  2014→Lookahead: 2
  2015→{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
  2016→— the enumerated variable (voicing
  2017→{axt_enumeration})
  2018→and the specific
  2019→{axt_enum_value}
  2020→that activates this gate.
  2021→The gate variable may belong to a different regime (cross-regime gating is legal).
  2022→
  2023→[[axhrgv_variable]]
  2024→{axhrgv_variable}::
  2025→Marks a variable within the current group in a subdocument.
  2026→Lookahead: 1
  2027→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  2028→— the variable being detailed within the current group scope.
  2029→
  2030→==== Nesting Rules
  2031→
  2032→Hierarchy markers establish implicit scope through document order:
  2033→
  2034→* Higher-level markers implicitly close previous scope (no end markers needed)
  2035→* {axhrgb_group},
  2036→{axhrgc_gate},
  2037→and
  2038→{axhrgv_variable}
  2039→must appear within
  2040→{axhrb_regime}
  2041→scope
  2042→* {axhrgc_gate}
  2043→and
  2044→{axhrgv_variable}
  2045→must appear within
  2046→{axhrgb_group}
  2047→scope
  2048→* Multiple
  2049→{axhrgc_gate}
  2050→within one group have AND semantics
  2051→
  2052→==== Cross-Document Validation
  2053→
  2054→* {axhrb_regime}
  2055→attribute reference must match a regime defined with
  2056→{axvr_regime}
  2057→in parent
  2058→* {axhrgb_group}
  2059→attribute reference must match a group defined with
  2060→{axvr_group}
  2061→under that regime
  2062→* {axhrgc_gate}
  2063→first attribute reference must voice
  2064→{axt_enumeration};
  2065→second must be an
  2066→{axt_enum_value}
  2067→of that specific enumeration type
  2068→
  2069→== Axial Type Universal Terms
  2070→
  2071→{axtu_*} types represent generic infrastructure patterns that are not specific to any cloud provider or platform.
  2072→These types capture critical gestalts that appear across many systems.
  2073→Platform-specific nuances (e.g., path separators on Windows vs Unix) are handled through
  2074→{axl_voicing_s}.
  2075→
  2076→[[axtu_string]]
  2077→{axtu_string}::
  2078→A
  2079→{axl_motif}
  2080→representing a generic bounded string value.
  2081→{axl_voicing_s} specify minimum and maximum length constraints appropriate to their context.
  2082→
  2083→[[axtu_path]]
  2084→{axtu_path}::
  2085→A
  2086→{axl_motif}
  2087→representing a file system path.
  2088→Platform differences (path separators, root conventions) are handled by
  2089→{axl_voicing_s}.
  2090→May be absolute or relative depending on context.
  2091→
  2092→[[axtu_ipv4]]
  2093→{axtu_ipv4}::
  2094→A
  2095→{axl_motif}
  2096→representing an IPv4 address in dotted-decimal notation.
  2097→Format: four octets 0-255 separated by dots (e.g., `192.168.1.1`).
  2098→
  2099→[[axtu_cidr]]
  2100→{axtu_cidr}::
  2101→A
  2102→{axl_motif}
  2103→representing a CIDR block for network addressing.
  2104→Format: IPv4 address followed by slash and prefix length (e.g., `10.0.0.0/8`).
  2105→
  2106→[[axtu_port]]
  2107→{axtu_port}::
  2108→A
  2109→{axl_motif}
  2110→representing a network port number.
  2111→Valid range: 1-65535. Well-known ports are 1-1023.
  2112→
  2113→[[axtu_domain]]
  2114→{axtu_domain}::
  2115→A
  2116→{axl_motif}
  2117→representing a DNS domain name.
  2118→Must start and end with alphanumeric characters; may contain dots and hyphens.
  2119→
  2120→[[axtu_sha256]]
  2121→{axtu_sha256}::
  2122→A
  2123→{axl_motif}
  2124→representing a SHA-256 cryptographic hash.
  2125→Format: 64 lowercase hexadecimal characters.
  2126→
  2127→[[axtu_xname]]
  2128→{axtu_xname}::
  2129→A
  2130→{axl_motif}
  2131→representing a cross-context safe identifier.
  2132→Must start with a letter and contain only letters, digits, underscores, and hyphens.
  2133→Safe for use in file names, environment variables, and most programming contexts.
  2134→
  2135→== Axial Type Google Terms
  2136→
  2137→{axtg_*} types represent Google Cloud Platform resource identifiers and patterns.
  2138→These types have specific format requirements enforced by GCP APIs.
  2139→
  2140→[[axtg_project_id]]
  2141→{axtg_project_id}::
  2142→A
  2143→{axl_motif}
  2144→representing a Google Cloud Platform project identifier.
  2145→Format: 6-30 lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens; must start with a letter and end with letter or digit.
  2146→Globally unique across all of GCP.
  2147→
  2148→[[axtg_region]]
  2149→{axtg_region}::
  2150→A
  2151→{axl_motif}
  2152→representing a Google Cloud Platform region code.
  2153→Format: lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens (e.g., `us-central1`, `europe-west1`).
  2154→Determines geographic location for regional resources.
  2155→
  2156→[[axtg_service_account]]
  2157→{axtg_service_account}::
  2158→A
  2159→{axl_motif}
  2160→representing a Google Cloud Platform service account email.
  2161→Format: `{account-id}@{project-id}.iam.gserviceaccount.com` where account-id is 6-30 characters.
  2162→Used as identity for programmatic API access.
  2163→
  2164→[[axtg_billing_account]]
  2165→{axtg_billing_account}::
  2166→A
  2167→{axl_motif}
  2168→representing a Google Cloud Platform billing account identifier.
  2169→Format: `XXXXXX-XXXXXX-XXXXXX` (three groups of six uppercase alphanumeric characters).
  2170→Links projects to payment sources.
  2171→
  2172→[[axtg_project_number]]
  2173→{axtg_project_number}::
  2174→A
  2175→{axl_motif}
  2176→representing a Google Cloud Platform project number.
  2177→Format: numeric identifier automatically assigned at project creation.
  2178→Globally unique across all of GCP.
  2179→Immutable once assigned.
  2180→
  2181→== Axial Infrastructure Google Terms
  2182→
  2183→{axig_*} terms represent Google Cloud Platform infrastructure patterns.
  2184→Each term explicitly declares its level:
  2185→
  2186→* **Service-level**: the managed platform itself
  2187→* **Instance-level**: a particular resource within a service
  2188→* **Operational**: execution patterns and states
  2189→
  2190→=== Service-Level Terms
  2191→
  2192→[[axig_resource_manager]]
  2193→{axig_resource_manager}::
  2194→A
  2195→{axl_motif}
  2196→representing Google Cloud Resource Manager.
  2197→Service-level: the platform for managing organizations, folders, and projects.
  2198→
  2199→[[axig_cloud_storage]]
  2200→{axig_cloud_storage}::
  2201→A
  2202→{axl_motif}
  2203→representing Google Cloud Storage.
  2204→Service-level: the object storage platform, not buckets within it.
  2205→
  2206→[[axig_artifact_registry]]
  2207→{axig_artifact_registry}::
  2208→A
  2209→{axl_motif}
  2210→representing Google Artifact Registry.
  2211→Service-level: the artifact management platform, not repositories within it.
  2212→
  2213→[[axig_cloud_build]]
  2214→{axig_cloud_build}::
  2215→A
  2216→{axl_motif}
  2217→representing Google Cloud Build.
  2218→Service-level: the build execution platform, not individual builds.
  2219→
  2220→[[axig_iam]]
  2221→{axig_iam}::
  2222→A
  2223→{axl_motif}
  2224→representing Google Cloud IAM.
  2225→Service-level: the identity and access management platform.
  2226→
  2227→=== Instance-Level: Resource Manager
  2228→
  2229→[[axig_organization]]
  2230→{axig_organization}::
  2231→A
  2232→{axl_motif}
  2233→representing a Google Cloud organization.
  2234→Instance-level: a particular organization, top of GCP resource hierarchy.
  2235→
  2236→[[axig_folder]]
  2237→{axig_folder}::
  2238→A
  2239→{axl_motif}
  2240→representing a Google Cloud folder.
  2241→Instance-level: an organizational container within an
  2242→{axig_organization}.
  2243→
  2244→[[axig_project]]
  2245→{axig_project}::
  2246→A
  2247→{axl_motif}
  2248→representing a Google Cloud project.

== Axial Regime Provenance Dimensions

Provenance dimensions qualify
{axrg_assignment}
to declare how values arrive at
{axvr_broach}
time.
These are valid dimensions on
{axvr_regime}
declaring which provenance(s) a regime supports.

[[axrd_file_sourced]]
{axrd_file_sourced}::
Assignments loaded from a persistent file via source or equivalent mechanism.
The file path is known at load time and the regime module is responsible for locating and sourcing it.

[[axrd_env_inherited]]
{axrd_env_inherited}::
Assignments received as environment variables from a parent process.
The regime module finds variables already in scope; no file sourcing occurs.

[[axrd_constructed]]
{axrd_constructed}::
Assignments computed or set programmatically before broach.
Values are assembled by application logic rather than loaded from a static source.
